title: Ringo Starr: Learning from Mistakes on Stage
summary: Ringo Starr is a famous drummer who was in The Beatles. He is 85 years old but still makes music. He made a new album called Long Long Road. Ringo likes to play music with other people because he says he learned from his mistakes when he played on stage. When he was a kid, he practiced drums alone, but his neighbors told him to stop because it was too loud. Now he tells his grandchildren to play music with friends. Ringo lives in Los Angeles and loves the warm weather. He also worked with a famous music maker named T Bone Burnett on his new album. Ringo says he wants to be called Ringo, not Sir, because he just wants to talk about music.

keywords.explanation: a person who plays the drums

options: Being a drummer in The Beatles

background_read: Ringo Starr was a drummer for The Beatles, one of the most famous bands ever. He is known for his catchphrase 'Peace and love.' He still makes music at age 85.
Article_Structure: WHO: Ringo Starr, a famous drummer from The Beatles.
WHAT: He made a new country album called Long Long Road.
WHERE: He lives in Los Angeles and recorded the album there and in Nashville.
WHY: He loves making music with others and learned from his mistakes on stage.

perspectives.description: He thinks making mistakes on stage is how you learn.
They thought his drumming was too loud.
